setup: HSiKAN forward+backward (sign-routed Chebyshev splines + highway gate over signed hyperedges, arity 3, d=16, cheb 6, 2 branches), 100 iters. Nagare = closed-form no-tape (SINGLE-THREADED, 0 rayon). PyTorch = same architecture with autograd, measured at 1 thread (per-core fair) and 6 threads (default). Peak RSS via /usr/bin/time -l.
note: PER-CORE (1 thread): Nagare HSiKAN is 2.3x FASTER than PyTorch (24 vs 52 ms @20k, 62 vs 140 @50k, 124 vs 283 @100k). MEMORY: Nagare uses 4-5x LESS peak RSS (86 vs 459 MB, 207 vs 843, 405 vs 1468) -- no autograd tape + no framework baseline. PyTorch only wins WALL-CLOCK by using 6 threads (Nagare is single-threaded -- parallelism left on the table). So on HSiKAN: Nagare wins per-core time AND memory decisively; PyTorch's only edge is multithreading Nagare hasn't used yet.
